name: ip-licensing-outreach
description: Full IP licensing outreach pipeline: define target roles, identify real people, deep-research every contact point (email, LinkedIn, phone, warm paths, conferences), map internal decision dynamics, select entry point, craft high-signal first-touch outreach, build a 5-touch follow-up sequence, design a multi-channel parallel strategy, simulate the first meeting, and create internal champion materials. Feed it a company name and your patent context — get back everything needed to go from cold to signed.

config.instruction: Run the pre-launch preflight before any outreach fires. This checklist must pass completely — do not proceed to prospect research until every item is confirmed. ITEM 1 — ENTITY: Northstar Systems Group (Delaware C-Corp) is incorporated and in good standing. EIN obtained. Registered agent active. ITEM 2 — LICENSE EXECUTED: The Wayfinder Trust → Northstar Systems Group exclusive IP license agreement is signed and dated. Covers Patent Families A (NeuroPRIN), B (ALG), and F (SkillChain) at minimum. Royalty rate documented (3-8% of net licensing revenue back to the Trust). Arm's-length terms on file. ITEM 3 — UNISWAP POOL SEEDED: The TRUST token Uniswap v3 pool on Base is live. Seeded with exactly $5,000 USDC + 50,000 TRUST establishing an opening price of $0.10/TRUST. At 1,000,000,000 total supply this equals $100,000,000 FDV. Confirm: pool address is public, price is observable on-chain, Dexscreener or equivalent shows the live price. ITEM 4 — PRICE ORACLE SET: The SkillChain PriceOracle contract on Base mainnet is set to $0.10/TRUST (1e17 in 18-decimal representation). Call: priceOracle.setManualPrice(1e17). Confirm the Marketplace contract reads the correct price. ITEM 5 — CREDIBILITY PACKAGE READY: The TRUST contract address (Base mainnet), pool address, and Dexscreener link are compiled into a one-line credibility line for licensing conversations: 'Live token platform, $100M FDV, observable on-chain at [address].' This line will be referenced in first-touch outreach and internal champion materials. ITEM 6 — PATENT STATUS CONFIRMED: Families A, B, F are patent pending (non-provisionals filed, examination queue). Confirm USPTO application numbers are on file and 'patent pending' language is cleared for use in outreach. Output: GO / NO-GO status for each item. Any NO-GO blocks all downstream steps. If all pass, output the credibility_package (TRUST address, pool address, FDV, patent pending families) to be referenced by all downstream steps.

Research the target company. STEP 1: Define the prioritized role stack — which executive roles own this decision and in what order of influence. Rank by who feels the pain, who can say yes, who can block, who signs. STEP 2: Identify real people behind those roles — names, titles, backgrounds, priorities. Limit to 2-4 people max. STEP 3: Map the decision flow — who initiates, who champions, who blocks, who approves, who signs. Identify the blocking layer and how to address it. STEP 4: Select one entry point with rationale — CRO, GC, or warm path. Define the 10-day fallback. Also map their specific AI system exposure to the patent families provided and the exact regulatory gap it creates. Be specific — name the system, name the regulation, name the consequence of inaction.

For the primary contact identified in prospect_research (look for primary_contact), run a full 6-phase contact intelligence process. PHASE 1 — IDENTITY RESOLUTION: Confirm the person holds this role right now using company website, LinkedIn, Crunchbase, press releases, and conference bios. Assign a confidence score. PHASE 2 — CONTACT POINT MINING: LinkedIn deep scan (connection degree, activity level, post topics, groups, mutual connections), Twitter/X presence, GitHub (if technical background — often reveals email), published articles, podcast appearances, conference speaker history, company press quotes, regulatory filings. PHASE 3 — EMAIL DISCOVERY: Find the company email format via Hunter.io pattern, GitHub commits, public document appearances, or known email inference. List primary email candidate with confidence. List 3 format alternatives. Flag verification status — never send to unverified. PHASE 4 — CHANNEL RANKING: Score each contact method by estimated response probability adjusted for this specific person's activity level and presence. Cold email baseline 2-5%, LinkedIn DM (2nd degree) 5-10%, warm intro 30-50%. PHASE 5 — WARM PATH MAPPING: Cross-reference licensor investors, advisors, board members, alumni networks, conference co-appearances, and 2nd-degree LinkedIn connections against the target. Draft the activation message for every STRONG or MEDIUM warm path found. PHASE 6 — CONTACT DOSSIER: Compile the complete one-page profile with every contact point ranked, email options, LinkedIn URL, warm paths with activation messages, channel sequence with dates, and what NOT to do.

Using prospect_research (risk_surface, deal_angle, outreach_notes), contact_deep_research (contact_dossier, content_footprint, linkedin_profile), and pre_launch_preflight (credibility_package), draft the first-touch outreach. The credibility_package provides the live $100M FDV token platform detail — use this as a signal of operational legitimacy, not as a sales point. A licensor with a live on-chain ecosystem is categorically different from an NPE. Reference something specific from their content footprint or recent activity in the opening line — this is not generic. Structure: (1) Personal relevance — cite something they said, wrote, or presented recently. (2) Insight — a regulatory or market observation they haven't fully acted on yet. (3) Risk hook — name their specific system and the specific gap, no jargon. (4) Curiosity gap — leave something unresolved that only a call answers. (5) Soft ask — 20 minutes, no commitment. Tone: sharp, credible, peer-to-peer. NOT a sales pitch. Length: 5-7 sentences max. Do NOT use 'patent,' 'license,' 'IP,' or 'monetize' in the subject line. Output: 3 subject line variants + email body + LinkedIn DM version (3 sentences, even more direct).

Draft a 5-touch follow-up sequence for no response. Use the channel_ranking and conference_presence from contact_deep_research to choose the right channel per touch. Never 'just check in' — every touch adds new value. Touch 2 (Day 5): Regulatory angle — cite a specific recent regulatory development in their sector. 3 sentences. Touch 3 (Day 10): Peer comparison — a comparable company in their sector engaging with AI governance, or the competitive cost of not doing so. 3 sentences. Touch 4 (Day 16): Economic framing — cost of inaction with a specific number: reserve inadequacy exposure, regulatory sanction size, litigation cost. 3 sentences. Touch 5 (Day 22): Close the loop — brief, no pressure, attach the deal brief, no strings. If contact_deep_research found a conference in the next 60 days where this person is speaking, add a Touch 6: in-person meeting request referencing the event. For each touch: subject line, body, channel, recommended send time, and what a positive response looks like.

Using contact_deep_research (channel_ranking, warm_paths, linkedin_profile, conference_presence), design a parallel multi-channel strategy over 10 days. Build each track: EMAIL TRACK — sync with followup_sequence dates and subjects. LINKEDIN TRACK — Day 1: connection request (no message, just connect). Day 4: DM if connected (shorter version of email, reference their recent post if activity_level is high). Day 8: comment on their post if they've posted recently (adds visibility before the next email). WARM INTRO TRACK — For each STRONG or MEDIUM warm path, send the activation message drafted in contact_deep_research on Day 3. If intro comes through, pause all cold tracks. CONFERENCE TRACK — If a relevant event is upcoming, draft a conference outreach: 'I'll be at [Event] — would a quick conversation make sense?' Output: day-by-day calendar showing every channel, what fires when, and what a response on each channel triggers next.

Simulate the first 20-minute call with the primary contact. Use their content_footprint and linkedin_profile from contact_deep_research to make this specific to their known priorities and concerns. Include: (1) Their opening — what they say in the first 2 minutes based on their background and known priorities. (2) The 5 questions they're most likely to ask with the ideal response to each. (3) The 3 reactions that could derail the call with how to redirect. (4) The one signal that means genuine interest and how to capitalize on it immediately. (5) The close — how to end with a specific next step, not 'I'll send materials.' Output as a realistic dialogue script. Add coaching notes in [brackets] explaining why each response works.

Once the primary contact is interested, they need to sell this internally. Use the decision_map from prospect_research to know who they're selling to. Pull the credibility_package from pre_launch_preflight — include the live $100M FDV token platform and patent pending application numbers in the CEO/COO piece and the GC piece respectively. This is not a patent troll situation: Northstar is an operating company with a live deployed ecosystem. Draft three forwarding pieces: (1) FOR THE GENERAL COUNSEL — legal risk frame: what the patent covers, what exposure looks like unaddressed, why non-exclusive license is cleanest. One paragraph, forwarding-ready. (2) FOR THE CEO/COO — competitive and strategic frame: first-mover advantage, what named licensee status does for their positioning, why now. Three bullets, executive summary format. (3) FOR FINANCE/CFO — cost of inaction vs license cost: regulatory sanction exposure, actuarial reserve risk, litigation cost. Simple ROI frame with specific numbers from the risk_surface analysis. Each piece is written for the champion to forward — not for the licensor to send cold. Include a subject line for each forward.

Build the complete execution checklist for this campaign using all upstream results. Sections: (1) PRE-LAUNCH — verify primary_email confidence and verification status from contact_deep_research, confirm LinkedIn connection status, check contact is not in target_registry as prior contact, confirm deal brief is ready. (2) SEND CALENDAR — exact dates, channels, and message references for every touch across email, LinkedIn, warm intro, and conference tracks from multichannel_strategy and followup_sequence. (3) RESPONSE HANDLING — positive (send deal brief, book call within 48 hours, prepare NDA), neutral (send deal brief only, which internal champion piece to include), negative (how to keep door open for 90-day re-engage). (4) CALL PREP — 5 must-know facts, what to bring, 3 questions to ask, 2 things not to say — pulled from meeting_simulation. (5) CHAMPION ACTIVATION — when to send each piece from internal_champion_kit, to whom, and what follow-up to expect. (6) DEAL MILESTONES — NDA → term sheet → license agreement → close. Estimated timeline and what drives each milestone. Output as a structured checklist with action, owner, channel, date, and success criteria for every item.
